## Top 3 Papers to Read

*(No new arXiv papers scan ran today — labs were quiet. Three highest-priority papers from recent scans that directly unblock this week's experiments:)*

1. **AHEAD: Action-Conditioned World Models for Embodied AI** — adds 4.9M params to a frozen 7B VLA for latent-space rollouts; 29/30 vs. 0/30 task success; this is the rollout engine for your bridge experiment; **you need to actually run this this week, not just know about it.**
   [arXiv:2606.02486](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02486) · Relevance: ★★★★★

2. **WorldOlympiad: A Multi-Track Benchmark for Video World Models** — three-track evaluation (physics, geometry, interactivity) that still does not measure policy utility; the gap it leaves is precisely your WMATS contribution; read the geometry track to sharpen your framing.
   [arXiv:2606.11129](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11129) · Relevance: ★★★★☆

3. **GraspGen-X: Contact Geometry for Manipulation Generalization** (ICRA 2026) — swept-volume contact geometry predicts manipulation generalization better than spatial coverage alone; provides theoretical grounding for the WM-readiness hypothesis (contact diversity > spatial diversity as the key data property).
   [arXiv:2606.00998](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.00998) · Relevance: ★★★☆☆

## GitHub Watch

- **[BPerju/LeVizAudit](https://github.com/BPerju/LeVizAudit)** (new, June 16) — audits spatial pose diversity in LeRobot demonstration datasets. Third data-quality tool in one week. Still no tool that measures WM-readiness. This is the gap your paper fills.

- **[parfum666/robot-data-eval-toolkit](https://github.com/parfum666/robot-data-eval-toolkit)** — episode statistics + reward analysis for robot datasets. Same story: BC-readiness metrics only, no WM downstream correlation. The Physical AI Dataset's force/torque logs go untouched.

- **[Rahul-Vinoth/PantheonDataPlatform](https://github.com/Rahul-Vinoth/PantheonDataPlatform)** — petabyte-scale canonical schema normalization for robot demonstrations. Signals that large-scale dataset curation infrastructure is arriving. **WM-readiness criteria need to exist before this pipeline decides what to keep.**

## Today's Best Research Idea

**"Visual Metric Blindness in Physical AI" — The 6-8 Week Paper**

Your sana-wm-pipeline already contains Level 1 evidence: PSNR gap ≈ 0 across GT-pose / GT-depth / Default modes, ATE gap ≈ 714,000×. Visual quality metrics are blind to geometric world model fidelity — you have 141 tests proving this. The one missing piece is Level 2: does the ATE gap predict downstream policy utility?

The bridge experiment answers this directly: take the two extreme sana-wm outputs (GT-pose = low ATE, Default = high ATE), run AHEAD rollouts on each, train Diffusion Policy on each rollout set, and evaluate on MuJoCo peg insertion (3 seeds, 5K and 10K steps). If GT-pose rollouts produce >15 percentage points higher task success, the Level-2 claim is confirmed and the paper structure is complete: sana-wm Level-1 finding + bridge experiment Level-2 finding = a full argument that visual metrics fail at every layer of the physical AI stack.

**This is a CoRL 2027 paper (April 2027 deadline) in 6-8 weeks, not a 6-month 54-run sweep.** The data is already in hand.

Quick experiment: Set up the AHEAD rollout pipeline on your existing sana-wm GT-pose and Default outputs this week. 24 H100 hours total. Start today.

## Your Day Plan

**Morning** (before anything else, ~20 min): Open `sana-wm-pipeline`, find the June 13 three-mode comparison outputs. Paste the following three sentences into a new file `Planning/WorldBench_Design.md`, then append the WMATS design stub from the June 15 briefing below them, then `git commit -m "design: WorldBench empirical seed + WMATS stub" && git push`:

> *"Empirical seed: sana-wm-pipeline three-mode validation (June 13, 2026) shows PSNR gap ≈ 0 across modes but ATE gap ≈ 714,000× between GT-pose and Default modes. This demonstrates that visual quality metrics are blind to geometric world model fidelity — the first empirical evidence that the WorldBench/WMATS thesis is correct. The bridge experiment (running Diffusion Policy on GT-pose vs. Default rollouts) will test whether the ATE gap predicts downstream policy success."*

**Afternoon** (~4 hrs setup): Wire up the bridge experiment. Pull AHEAD open weights (arXiv:2606.02486). Confirm your sana-wm GT-pose and Default outputs are staged. Write the AHEAD inference script on each. Start the run on H100x1.

**Evening** (~1 hr): Queue the contact frequency probe in parallel. Pull NVIDIA Physical AI Dataset from HuggingFace. Label 1,000 trajectories by force-threshold event count. Split Q1 vs. Q4 (250 trajectories each). Start a ≤50M param world model training run. This is 6 H100 hours overnight — let it run while you sleep.

**This Week's Goal**: By Friday June 20 — three artifacts: (1) `WorldBench_Design.md` committed, (2) sana-wm bridge experiment running or complete, (3) contact frequency probe result in hand. Zero to three in five days.

## Research Radar

★ **Heating up**: Robot demonstration data quality tooling (three tools in one week — LeVizAudit, robot-data-eval-toolkit, PantheonDataPlatform). The field is building the curation pipeline before anyone defines what "good for world models" means. First-mover advantage on WM-readiness criteria is measured in weeks, not months.

★ **Heating up**: Action-conditioned world model rollouts as a policy training substrate (AHEAD 29/30 tasks). The question shifts from "can a world model generate video?" to "can world model rollouts substitute for real environment interaction?" Your bridge experiment sits at this frontier.

☆ **Opportunity**: WM-readiness as a data property — no definition, no metric, no paper. The Physical AI Dataset's force/torque logs are open, 4.8M+ downloads, and completely unstudied for world model pretraining quality. Entry point: the contact frequency probe.

☆ **Opportunity**: The 30-second world model frontier (Cosmos Predict 2.5's documented ceiling). OpenAI named the biological mechanism (offline sleep-consolidation replay) in their Dreaming memory system — the same mechanism that could extend world model coherence beyond 30 seconds. This is a 6-month research direction to begin designing now.

⚠ **Crowded**: LeRobot integrations (three this week: eco65-pnp, Lerobot-Jaka, and the broader ecosystem). A GitHub contribution, not a paper. The community will have 30+ integrations by end of 2026.

⚠ **Crowded**: VLM evaluation harnesses (`abhijeetgupta02/vlm-eval-harness` joins EleutherAI lm-eval, HELM, VLMEvalKit). New harnesses need to solve a structural gap, not add unified logging.

⚠ **Deprioritize this week**: `Yimi81/awesome-spatial-reasoning-scene-generation` and any landscape list. The landscape is well-understood. Reading more is not the bottleneck. **Writing what you already know and running experiments on data you already have is.**
